description: ¬ß6.2 Shape 4 typed no-RHS canonical-empty defaults (RT): `<count>: int`‚Üí0, `<name>: string`‚Üí"", `<active>: bool`‚Üífalse, `<todos>: string[]`‚Üí[]. No initializer, no input ‚Äî the cell inits to the type's canonical empty DEFINED value. State snapshot + interpolations both read the empties; `@todos.length` is 0.
rationale: ¬ß6.2 Shape 4 (typed no-RHS): a type-annotated cell with no initializer defaults to the type's canonical empty DEFINED value ‚Äî int‚Üí0, string‚Üí"", bool‚Üífalse, T[]‚Üí[]. `<x>: T` is exact sugar for `<x>: T = <canonical-empty>`. These are DEFINED values (¬ß42.1.1), not absence, so reads are legal with no lifecycle.
